description: SnoopTag: a 12-aa peptide split from the D4 domain of Streptococcus pneumoniae RrgA that spontaneously forms a covalent isopeptide bond with its partner protein SnoopCatcher (>99% yield) and is orthogonal to SpyTag/SpyCatcher.

references.authors: Veggiani G, Nakamura T, Brenner MD, Gayet RV, Yan J, Robinson CV, Howarth M
references.title: Programmable polyproteams built using twin peptide superglues
references.journal: Proc Natl Acad Sci U S A (2016)

references.authors: Buldun CM, Jean JX, Bedford MR, Howarth M
references.title: SnoopLigase Catalyzes Peptide-Peptide Locking and Enables Solid-Phase Conjugate Isolation

references.authors: Keeble AH, Yadav VK, Ferla MP, Bauer CC, Chuntharpursat-Bon E, Huang J, Bon RS, Howarth M
references.title: DogCatcher allows loop-friendly protein-protein ligation

references.authors: Keeble AH, Wood DP, Howarth M
references.title: Design and Evolution of Enhanced Peptide-Peptide Ligation for Modular Transglutaminase Assembly

references.authors: Bruun TUJ, Andersson AC, Draper SJ, Howarth M
references.title: Engineering a Rugged Nanoscaffold To Enhance Plug-and-Display Vaccination

label: SnoopTag
note: 12-aa reactive peptide split from the D4 (CnaA-like) domain of Streptococcus pneumoniae RrgA pilus adhesin (UniProt A7KT94, residues 727–738). Pairs with the protein SnoopCatcher to form a spontaneous, covalent intermolecular isopeptide bond. Orthogonal to the SpyTag/SpyCatcher pair. Sequence verified 100% over 12 aa against GenBank AMD39968.1 (Veggiani 2016 PNAS deposit).

provenance.sequence_source: AMD39968.1 (GenBank protein, "SnoopTag-MBP [synthetic construct]", deposited 10-FEB-2016; Veggiani et al. 2016, PNAS 113:1202–1207, PMID 26787909): SnoopTag KLGDIEFIKVNK at residues 23–34 (1-based), 100% identity over 12 aa. Biological origin confirmed in UniProt A7KT94 (RrgA, S. pneumoniae, residues 727–738). Independently fetched and compared.

functional_claims.label: Spontaneous covalent isopeptide-bond formation with SnoopCatcher

value.mechanism: Reactive peptide split from the RrgA D4 domain; on mixing with the partner protein SnoopCatcher it reconstitutes the split domain and forms a spontaneous intramolecular-derived intermolecular isopeptide (amide) bond between a Lys side chain on SnoopTag and an Asn side chain on SnoopCatcher, covalently and irreversibly locking the two units together.

source.quote: By engineering the adhesin RrgA from Streptococcus pneumoniae, we developed the peptide SnoopTag, which formed a spontaneous isopeptide bond to its protein partner SnoopCatcher with >99% yield and no cross-reaction to SpyTag/SpyCatcher. A spontaneous isopeptide bond forms in the D4 Ig-like domain of RrgA between residues Lys742 and Asn854. We split the D4 domain in the loop following the N-terminal β-strand and, after exploring various extensions and truncations of the two partners, settled on the peptide tag we termed SnoopTag (residues 734–745) and the protein partner we named SnoopCatcher (residues 749–860). Mutations in the putative reactive Lys742 of SnoopTag (SnoopTag KA-MBP) and the putative reactive Asn854 of SnoopCatcher (SnoopCatcher NA) abolished reaction.

functional_claims.usefulness_rationale: Describes the core functional mechanism (isopeptide bond between Lys and Asn) and partner (SnoopCatcher), directly actionable for designers choosing and deploying this part; specifies reaction chemistry and irreversibility, differentiates from SpyTag.

functional_claims.label: SnoopTag + SnoopCatcher react to >99% yield

value.readout: isopeptide bond formation between SnoopTag and SnoopCatcher
value.note: Reported reaction completeness for the SnoopTag/SnoopCatcher pair.

source.quote: SnoopTag, which formed a spontaneous isopeptide bond to its protein partner SnoopCatcher with >99% yield and no cross-reaction to SpyTag/SpyCatcher

functional_claims.usefulness_rationale: Reaction efficiency and completeness are operationally critical: a designer selecting SnoopTag/SnoopCatcher needs to know if the reaction will go to completion with their intended stoichiometry and conditions. This determines whether they can use the system reliably in polyprotein assembly.
functional_claims.comment: superseded by performance_yield__v2: The abstract states '>99% yield' but experimental data shows this is conditional on stoichiometry: 1:1 ratio gives only ~80%, while excess partner drives near-complete reaction. The exact percentage for 'quantitative' conversion is not numerically specified beyond 'almost quantitative' in discussion."

functional_claims.label: Orthogonal to SpyTag/SpyCatcher (no cross-reaction)

source.quote: SnoopTag/SnoopCatcher reacted with each other but not with an alternative peptide/protein pair, SpyTag/SpyCatcher. ... Each partner reacted efficiently with its cognate pair, but we found no trace of cross-reaction, even after overnight incubation (Fig. 2C).

functional_claims.usefulness_rationale: Directly discriminates SnoopTag from SpyTag in design choice; engineers MUST know about orthogonality when selecting which tag system to use for polyprotein assembly where multiple tags are required.

functional_claims.label: Derived from the RrgA pilus adhesin D4 domain of S. pneumoniae

value.derivation: split-domain engineering of the RrgA isopeptide-bond-forming domain into a peptide (SnoopTag) and protein (SnoopCatcher) pair

source.quote: By engineering the adhesin RrgA from [Streptococcus pneumoniae], we developed the peptide SnoopTag

functional_claims.usefulness_rationale: Discriminates the part's source organism and parent protein structure (explains isopeptide-bond mechanism origin), but specific residue coordinates have low operational utility since designers use the final 12-aa sequence, not parent coordinates.
functional_claims.comment: The paper clearly specifies that after optimization, SnoopTag comprises residues 734-745 of RrgA D4, not 727-738; this is a straightforward factual correction from the Methods section.

functional_claims.label: Programmable, irreversible protein-protein conjugation and modular assembly

applications: covalent fusion of two protein modules
sequential / programmable polyprotein ('polyproteam') assembly when combined orthogonally with SpyTag/SpyCatcher

source.quote: Solid-phase attachment followed by sequential SpyTag or SnoopTag reaction between building-blocks enabled iterative extension. Linear, branched, and combinatorial polyproteins were synthesized

functional_claims.usefulness_rationale: Concrete, selection-relevant applications (irreversible covalent assembly, programmable polyproteams, modular display) with operationally actionable details (solid-phase attachment, sequential iterative reactions, orthogonality with SpyTag/SpyCatcher). Directly addresses how an engineer would use SnoopTag.

functional_claims.label: RrgA scaffold is evolvable: SnoopTagJr, DogTag, SnoopTag2 derivatives

variants: SnoopTagJr (three-part RrgA split with DogTag + SnoopLigase)
value.note: All derived from the same S. pneumoniae RrgA scaffold; these are distinct related parts, not this exact peptide.

source.quote: Three-part RrgA split generating SnoopTagJr, DogTag, and SnoopLigase

functional_claims.usefulness_rationale: The claim identifies related RrgA-derived variants useful for understanding the SnoopTag ecosystem and evolutionary development, but includes unverified SnoopTag2 which is not supported by the cited 2018 paper.
functional_claims.comment: superseded by sequence_variant__v2: SnoopTag2 is not mentioned in the cited 2018 Buldun et al. paper; SnoopTag2 likely derives from a different publication not cited here.

functional_claims.label: SnoopTag + SnoopCatcher react quantitatively to completion with excess partner (stoichiometry-dependent)

value.readout: isopeptide bond formation between SnoopTag and SnoopCatcher
value.note: Reaction completeness is stoichiometry-dependent: ~80% at 1:1 molar ratio; quantitative (~95%+) conversion when either partner is in twofold excess. Quantitatively reacted SnoopCatcher was ~100% consumed with excess SnoopTag-MBP.

source.quote: With a 1:1 SnoopCatcher to SnoopTag-MBP, ∼80% of SnoopTag-MBP reacted. However, with a twofold excess of SnoopCatcher, SnoopTag-MBP reacted quantitatively (Fig. 1E and Fig. S1B). Similarly with an excess of SnoopTag-MBP, SnoopCatcher was ∼100% consumed (Fig. 1F and Fig. S1C).

functional_claims.usefulness_rationale: Reaction efficiency and completeness are operationally critical: a designer selecting SnoopTag/SnoopCatcher needs to know if the reaction will go to completion with their intended stoichiometry and conditions. This determines whether they can use the system reliably in polyprotein assembly.

functional_claims.label: RrgA scaffold derivatives from Buldun et al. 2018: SnoopTagJr, DogTag, SnoopLigase

variants: SnoopTagJr (improved SnoopTag with D737S, 12 amino acids, residues 734-745)
SnoopLigase (catalytic ligase, residues 743-846 with A808P Q837P D838G I839V mutations)
value.note: All derived from three-part splitting of S. pneumoniae RrgA C-terminal domain (Buldun et al. 2018 JACS). SnoopTag2 is not described in this reference.

source.quote: We initially developed these components by three-part splitting of the Streptococcus pneumoniae adhesin RrgA. The units were then engineered, guided by structure, bioinformatic analysis of sequence homology, and computational prediction of stability.

functional_claims.usefulness_rationale: The claim identifies related RrgA-derived variants useful for understanding the SnoopTag ecosystem and evolutionary development, but includes unverified SnoopTag2 which is not supported by the cited 2018 paper.
